_meta.description: SageReasoning Compliance Register — maps external regulatory obligations to mandate rules

obligations.notes: EU AI Office published Art. 6 classification guidelines in Feb 2026. EU Digital Omnibus proposes postponing high-risk obligations by up to 16 months (backstop Dec 2027 for Annex III). SageReasoning evaluates reasoning quality, not persons — profiling exemption likely applies. FOUNDER ACTION REQUIRED: Review Art. 6 guidelines to confirm SageReasoning's agent trust layer is not classified as high-risk under Annex III (particularly biometric/profiling categories).
actions: PRIORITY: Review Feb 2026 Art. 6 classification guidelines against agent trust layer
Monitor Digital Omnibus legislative progress — if adopted before Aug 2026, high-risk deadline extends
Assess whether agent trust layer constitutes profiling under Art. 6
Review R3 disclaimer adequacy against Art. 50 Code of Practice v2 (March 2026)

obligations.notes: Second Draft Code of Practice for Art. 50 (marking/labelling AI-generated content) published March 2026. R3 disclaimer satisfies Art. 50 transparency. API responses identify AI-generated content. No action required at this time — confirm alignment at next review when final Code of Practice is published.
actions: Confirm R3 disclaimer text meets final Art. 50 Code of Practice when published
Monitor whether watermarking/labelling requirements extend to text-based evaluation outputs

obligations.obligation: EU AI Act — GPAI Model Obligations
obligations.notes: SageReasoning uses third-party GPAI (Claude); does not train or distribute its own foundation model. Digital Omnibus may clarify downstream deployer obligations. Monitor.
actions: Monitor for downstream deployer obligation guidance from EU AI Office
Monitor Digital Omnibus for deployer-specific GPAI obligations

obligations.notes: VAISS remains voluntary. Dec 2025 National AI Plan confirmed no mandatory conversion at this time. Australian AI Safety Institute (AISI) becoming operational in early 2026 — monitoring for enforcement signals. No status change required.
actions: Monitor AISI publications once operational for enforcement signals
Track outcome of mandatory guardrails consultation for high-risk settings
Review VAISS × Guidance for AI Adoption crosswalk alignment

obligations.external_reference: Privacy Act 1988, APPs 1–13; Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024
obligations.notes: MAJOR CHANGE: Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024 passed Dec 2024. Automated decision-making transparency obligations take effect 10 Dec 2026. Businesses using AI to make decisions that could significantly affect individuals must disclose this in privacy policies. Children's Online Privacy Code also due by Dec 2026. FOUNDER ACTION REQUIRED: Privacy policy must be updated before Dec 2026 to comply with APP 1.7 automated decision-making transparency. Age verification for under-16 users needs review against Children's Online Privacy Code.
actions: PRIORITY: Update privacy policy for APP 1.7 automated decision-making transparency before Dec 2026
Review Children's Online Privacy Code requirements when OAIC publishes draft
Assess whether age verification gate (currently under-16 parental consent) meets Code requirements
Implement /api/user/export and /api/user/delete endpoints (existing deferred item)
Obtain lawyer review of privacy policy before Dec 2026 deadline

obligations.external_reference: Competition and Consumer Act 2010, Sch 2, Part 2-1
obligations.notes: ACCC 2026-27 enforcement priorities include AI-related digital market conduct and ghost websites using generative AI. No direct enforcement actions against AI evaluation services found. R3 disclaimer continues to prevent misleading conduct (s18) and R9 prevents false representations (s29). No status change required.
actions: Monitor ACCC enforcement actions against AI evaluation services
Review marketing materials against s18/s29 at each review cycle

obligations.notes: No updates to the standard itself in Q1 2026. New implementation guide published Feb 2026 — useful for gap analysis when pursuing certification. Manifest rules map to several Annex A controls. Full certification not pursued at launch.
actions: Map R1–R14 to specific Annex A control objectives using Feb 2026 implementation guide
Identify gaps between current controls and ISO 42001 requirements
Reassess certification value when revenue exceeds $50K/year

obligations.notes: NIST Cyber AI Profile under development (comment period closed, initial public draft expected 2026). AI RMF 1.1 addenda and expanded profiles expected through 2026. No material change to RMF 1.0 core. No status change required.
actions: Monitor NIST Cyber AI Profile publication
Adopt NIST AI RMF vocabulary in compliance documentation
Review AI RMF 1.1 addenda when published

obligations.external_reference: Regulation 2016/679, Art. 5, 6, 9, 22, 35; proposed Digital Omnibus amendments
obligations.notes: EU Digital Omnibus proposes legitimate interest basis for AI development/operation and new exemption for residual special-category data. If adopted, this may simplify GDPR compliance for SageReasoning's AI processing. GDPR delete/export endpoints remain a deferred item (Security Audit D8). No EU marketing at launch. Status unchanged — implement before EU expansion.
actions: Monitor Digital Omnibus GDPR amendments progress through EU Parliament
Implement GDPR delete/export endpoints (Security Audit D8)
Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment before EU marketing
Assess Art. 22 applicability to agent evaluation outputs
Evaluate whether legitimate interest basis (if adopted) applies to sage-reason processing

obligations.notes: ASIC Moneysmart published AI financial guidance March 2026 — warns consumers about trusting AI for financial decisions. ASIC emphasises existing AFSL obligations apply fully to AI-powered tools. Reinforces need for lawyer review before sage-invest marketplace listing. No status change.
actions: Obtain Australian financial services lawyer opinion on sage-invest
Do not list sage-invest on marketplace until lawyer review complete
Monitor ASIC guidance on AI-generated financial product advice
Review sage-invest disclaimers against ASIC Moneysmart guidance

obligations.notes: Business plan allocates ~$1,200/yr. Not yet purchased. Must be in place before public launch.
actions: Obtain professional indemnity insurance quotes
Ensure policy covers AI-generated philosophical evaluation errors
Purchase before public launch

obligations.notes: Business plan allocates ~$600/yr. Not yet purchased. Must be in place before public launch.
actions: Obtain cyber insurance quotes
Ensure policy covers Supabase-hosted data and API breach scenarios
Purchase before public launch

obligations.notes: Patent pending on philosophical evaluation framework architecture. File before public API launch.
actions: Prepare patent application for philosophical evaluation framework
File Q2 2026
Monitor competitor 'Stoic AI' claims quarterly

obligations.external_reference: Terms of Service, new Embedding section
obligations.notes: R13 created. ToS embedding section drafted in Policy Amendments doc. Not yet implemented in code.
actions: Implement embedding platform ToS section in website/src/app/terms/page.tsx
Add R13 compliance check to API onboarding flow
Create embedding platform agreement template

obligations.notes: Compliance matrix created (6A in Marketplace Compliance Amendment). 9/16 skills pass. 3 fail, 4 edge cases documented.
actions: Run compliance matrix for each new skill before marketplace listing
Resolve 4 edge case skills (sage-invest, sage-compliance, sage-negotiate, sage-coach)
Implement automated R10/R12 checks in skill submission pipeline
